description: Validation-only slice: add an end-to-end validation suite that (1) compares prompt versions, (2) measures narrative + outcome metrics, and (3) computes betting ROI from imported odds (manual CSV) for standardized actions. No product UI, no Telegram automation.

userStories.title: Define a single Validation Report schema + prompt-version leaderboard
userStories.description: As the founder, I need one canonical report that compares prompt versions and shows whether we are improving or wasting time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a JSON schema for validation output including: narrative metrics, outcome metrics (W/D/L), confidence calibration stats, and (if available) ROI metrics
ValidationEngine produces a leaderboard by prompt_version for a given season/league/round range
Report saved to disk as validation_report.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add odds storage + manual import (CSV) for time-travel-safe ROI validation
userStories.description: As the validation suite, I need pre-kickoff odds so I can compute ROI without building a live odds integration yet.
acceptanceCriteria: Add table odds_snapshots: id, fixture_id, market_key, selection_key, odds_decimal, captured_at, source
Add script scripts/import_odds_csv.py that imports 1X2 odds from a CSV and maps each row to fixture_id
Import enforces captured_at < fixture kickoff time/date (time-travel safe); invalid rows are rejected with clear logs
Migration runs successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Standardize 'action' extraction from analysis_reports for backtesting
userStories.description: As the validation suite, I need to turn each analysis report into a consistent action object so ROI and accuracy can be measured deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/validation/action_extractor.py that parses analysis_reports.full_json into a normalized action object
Supported outputs: NO_ACTION, AVOID_PUBLIC_SIDE, BET_1X2 (HOME|DRAW|AWAY) with referenced odds key
If report lacks a parseable action, extractor returns NO_ACTION (fails safe)
Unit tests cover at least 10 parsing cases across existing prompt versions
Typecheck/tests pass

userStories.title: Compute ROI, drawdown, and coverage metrics per prompt_version
userStories.description: As the founder, I need to know if any version is profitable (or at least not terrible) and how often it actually produces bets.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend ValidationEngine to compute betting_metrics per prompt_version: total_actions, total_bets, bet_rate, avg_odds, win_rate, ROI, max_drawdown
ROI uses imported odds_snapshots + actual results from fixtures/match_reality
If odds are missing for a bet, that bet is excluded and counted as 'unpriced_bet' in the report
Output included in validation_report.json under betting_metrics
Typecheck/tests pass

userStories.title: Add baselines: coin-flip W/D/L and bookmaker-implied baseline (if odds exist)
userStories.description: As the founder, I need baselines so we stop fooling ourselves about progress and know what 'good' means.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation report includes outcome baselines: random baseline (W/D/L uniform) and majority-class baseline
If 1X2 odds exist, add bookmaker baseline accuracy (pick lowest-odds outcome) and bookmaker expected ROI (should be negative)
Baselines appear side-by-side with each prompt_version
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: One command to run the suite across a round range (reproducible)
userStories.description: As the founder, I need one CLI command that runs everything and outputs a report I can trust and compare over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add scripts/run_validation_suite.py --season --league --from-round --to-round --prompt-version (optional) that runs validation end-to-end
Command outputs: validation_report.json and a short validation_report.md summary with the leaderboard
Run is deterministic given the same DB state (no new LLM calls unless explicitly requested)
Typecheck/tests pass

userStories.title: Guardrails: time-travel correctness tests for all new validation computations
userStories.description: As the system, I must guarantee we never accidentally use future data when validating, otherwise all results are worthless.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests that assert: odds captured_at < fixture date, context features only use matches before fixture date, and validation never reads post-match fields when generating actions
Validation fails loudly with an explicit error if any time-travel violation is detected
Typecheck/tests pass
